evidence: STACK ATL01 campus (T5/STACK) originally built 2012 via Fulton County Development Authority bonds; ATL01-2 is a neighbouring expansion at Webb Bridge Rd Alpharetta announced around 2019 acquisition — no standalone press release found for this second building

MetroNexus/CoreLocation bought 1033 Jefferson St NW in 2000 and invested $80M to convert the former Sears warehouse as MTCA; QTS acquired in Oct 2006 — first public signal for the facility as a data center is 2000
Facebook announced Social Circle / Newton County data center campus on March 7 2018; first phase (Social Circle 1) came online 2021
Second building part of same $1B campus announced March 2018; Facebook expansion disclosed in 2020 press release citing 1.5M sqft addition
Ongoing Meta Newton campus buildout — later phases trace to same 2018 announcement; no separate announcement found for phase 3 specifically
Same Meta Newton campus; phase 4 part of continuing expansion from 2018 original announcement; no separate press release found
Switch Q2 2022 earnings confirmed TAHOE RENO 2 under construction and pre-sold; opened H1 2023 per guidance; S&P records 2024 op year
Switch Q2 2022 10-Q discloses site development for TAHOE RENO 3/4/5; first public signal of TR4 is the 2022 campus expansion announcement
Switch announced $1B Citadel Campus in Storey County on Jan 16 2015; groundbreaking Aug 2015; Tahoe Reno 1 opened Feb 2017

Core Scientific's Marble facility (former American Thread plant) launched in 2018 as first facility; S&P lists a separate 69MW Marble 2 — no standalone announcement found; likely same 2017-2018 site-acquisition/permit period as Marble 1
Bitmain announced Rockdale blockchain data center on Aug 6 2018 via BusinessWire; 50MW facility opened Oct 2019; later expanded toward 300MW+
Whinstone US broke ground Nov 2019 on world's largest Bitcoin mine at Rockdale; acquired by Riot Blockchain May 2021; S&P records second facility (450MW) as operational 2021
